summary: Matched the Purin/Jigglypuff SpecialN implementation in src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN.c, adding full C for key charge-release animation and collision routines and tightening several near-matches in release/turn physics and animation. The decomp.dev report credited 9 new matches and +7480 matched bytes, with one small .sdata2 regression noted.

key_files.role: Only changed file; matched Purin SpecialN charge/release animation, physics, and collision code.

what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed `ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim`, including scale restoration/stepping via `ftPr_Init_803D05C8`, hit capsule toggling, roll-angle updates, and transition logic through `ftPr_SpecialS_8013DA24`.

Implemented previously stubbed `ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Coll`, handling wall collision masks `0x3F` and `0xFC0`, wall bounce velocity reversal/scaling via `da->xD4`, ground-to-air transition to `ftPr_MS_SpecialAirNChargeRelease`, callback setup, and roll/rotation reset.

Implemented previously stubbed `ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Coll`, including directional collision checks with `ft_8008239C`, wall bounce handling, landing transition to `ftPr_MS_SpecialNRelease`, ground velocity restoration, landing effect spawn `0x3FF`, and cliff-catch handling.

Added helper inlines `ftPr_JObjSetRotationY`, `ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim_inline`, `setFacingDir`, `wallBounceEffect`, and `ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Coll_inline` to express repeated code in forms that matched.
Replaced the local `NEGABS` macro with project `ABS` usages and added a `SIGNF` macro for converting stick/velocity signs to `1.0f` or `-1.0f`.

Adjusted near-matching functions including `ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Anim`, `ftPr_SpecialNRelease_IASA`, `ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Phys`, `ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Phys`, `ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Phys`, and `ftPr_SpecialAirNStartTurn_Phys` with codegen-sensitive local ordering, stack padding, comparisons, and ABS/sign forms.
Changed some effect spawn argument shapes, notably passing an angle pointer to `efSync_Spawn` in turn/landing effect paths rather than reusing a `Vec3` scratch value.
smart_moves: Used small static inline helpers to isolate awkward codegen patterns while keeping the main routines readable.

Manually implemented `ftPr_JObjSetRotationY` with the same assert checks and dirty-matrix behavior as the JObj path, including the unusual `(char*) &base[8]` assert argument needed by the generated code.

Factored common wall-hit visual/audio/camera behavior into `wallBounceEffect`, covering `efSync_Spawn(0x406)`, `Camera_80030E44`, `ftCommon_8007EBAC`, and `ft_PlaySFX` without duplicating the matched sequence.
Kept unknown motion flag bitmasks as local static `mf` constants with `@todo Named flags` instead of inventing names prematurely.
Used `PAD_STACK`, explicit temporary pads, split declarations, and assignment-inside-condition idioms where the diff shows those were needed for matching.
Captured reusable facing-direction cleanup in `setFacingDir`, matching the repeated sequence that applies `mv.pr.specialn.facing_dir`, clears it, and resets `xC`.
decomp_lessons: For fighter move files, matching may depend heavily on declaration order, temporary variable lifetimes, and whether locals are initialized at declaration or assigned later.
Prefer existing project macros like `ABS` when they produce the right codegen; add narrow helper macros like `SIGNF` only when the sign-selection expression recurs and matches.
Small inline wrappers can be a better matching tactic than forcing all logic directly into a large function, especially around HSD/JObj calls and repeated state cleanup.
Do not over-name unknown motion-state flag masks; preserving raw bitmasks with a clear TODO is safer until flag semantics are known.
Effect calls may require exact argument forms, such as passing an `f32*` angle, not just equivalent-looking scratch storage.
Wall-collision code often needs direction-specific env flag masks and ECB offsets; keeping the left/right paths explicit can match better than over-generalizing.

The PR body explicitly says the match required iteration with Claude and help from the permuter, so future difficult fighter matches should combine AI-generated candidates with permutation/testing rather than relying on a single rewrite.
naming_conventions: Existing public function names use the `ftPr_SpecialN...` and `ftPr_SpecialAirN...` prefixes for Purin grounded/aerial SpecialN states.

Private helper functions added for one file use `static inline`; some preserve the file prefix, such as `ftPr_SpecialAirNChargeRelease_Anim_inline`, while generic helpers use descriptive lowerCamelCase like `setFacingDir` and `wallBounceEffect`.
The new sign macro follows macro casing as `SIGNF`, paralleling existing uppercase utility macros such as `ABS`.
Unidentified fighter move fields remain as offsets like `x0`, `x8`, `x14`, `x18`, `x2C`, `x34.x`, and attribute offsets like `da->xD4`; the PR did not attempt speculative field renaming.
Unknown motion flag constants are kept in local `static u32 const mf` variables with `@todo Named flags` comments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(16)`, and explicit `u8` pad arrays when stack layout is codegen-sensitive.
Move declarations out of initializers when needed, e.g. declare `ftPurinAttributes* da;` then assign `da = fp->dat_attrs;`.
Assignment inside comparisons, such as `if (ABS(fp->gr_vel) > (max_vel = da->x4C))`, can matter for register allocation and matching.
Use `0.0f` float literals in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` arguments where the diff changed integer zeroes to float zeroes.
Sometimes re-read struct fields in conditions, e.g. checking `fp->coll_data.floor.normal.x > 0.0f` after storing an ABS slope value.
Nested scopes around `Vec3 pos` and `f32 angle` in collision paths help control variable lifetime and stack placement.
A direct JObj rotation-y helper can match where a general `ftPartSetRotY` call does not, but it must include assert and `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty` behavior.
Use exact constants and masks seen in the matched code: `0.017453292f`, `M_PI_2`, `M_PI`, `0x40012`, effect IDs `0x3FF` and `0x406`, and env masks `0x3F`/`0xFC0`.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
The PR author stated: "Took a lot of iteration with Claude, and some help from the permuter, but we got there in the end!"
decomp.dev reported matched code improved to 59.65% for GALE01, +0.19% and +7480 bytes.
decomp.dev also reported one unmatched `.sdata2` regression of -2 bytes, from 98.81% to 96.51%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `ftPr_SpecialN.c` to see whether the reported `.sdata2` regression was resolved.
Search for other fighter SpecialN collision stubs that can reuse the direction-specific wall bounce pattern from `wallBounceEffect`.
Search for remaining `@todo Named flags` in fighter move files and identify the shared motion flag bitmask used here.
Audit other `efSync_Spawn` call sites for cases where passing an angle pointer instead of a scratch vector improves matching.
Compare other HSD JObj rotation helper patterns to see when manual assert-preserving wrappers are needed.
